hero.tagPill: Range Rover Sport • 2005–Present • 3 Generations • TDV6, SDV6, TDV8, SDV8, V8, Ingenium
hero.h1: Range Rover Sport Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every generation. Every engine code. Every known failure. Every replacement cost — and the honest maths on when a Sport is worth saving. Backed by 711+ real UK Range Rover Sport enquiries across three generations [EM-VERIFIED].

trustStrip.label: 711+ Enquiries (2025) — Most-Enquired Performance Model in Our Data

ownershipVerdict.h2: Range Rover Sport — The Ownership Verdict

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The Tightest Maths in the Range — L320 values of £5,000–£12,000 against £5,000–£9,500 recons frequently fail our 60% rule; L494s mostly clear it; L461s clear it effortlessly [EM-QUOTE]
★★★☆☆ — the hardest-driven Land Rover: identical engines to the full-size car, shorter real-world lifespans in our enquiry patterns
2.7 TDV6 (276DT) — oil pump (L320) • 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (306DT) — crankshaft bearing (L320/L494)
Late L494 SDV8 or 2021+ D350 — the strongest engines in the cheapest wrappers they'll ever wear
L461 D350 — Ingenium six, no V6 legacy, warranty-backed

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The Range Rover Sport shares every engine with the full-size car but none of the gentle life — it's driven harder, traded cheaper, and sits closer to the repair-or-scrap line than any other Range Rover, which is exactly why the engine question matters more here than anywhere else.

atAGlance.h2: Range Rover Sport at a Glance

generations.h2: Range Rover Sport Generations — Find Your Vehicle
generations.subHeadline: Three generations, and the value curve decides as much as the engine does.

cards.title: Range Rover Sport L320
cards.verdict: Now the bargain-bin Range Rover — and the danger zone. Values have fallen to where one V6 diesel failure writes the car off. The TDV8 is the survivor's choice; any 2.7 without oil-pump history is a gamble, not a purchase.

cards.title: Range Rover Sport L494
cards.verdict: The definitive Sport — aluminium body, huge choice, real depreciation absorbed. Buy the SDV8 or a 2020+ D350 and it's excellent; buy a high-mileage SDV6 without oil-interval paperwork and you've bought the range's most common £9,500 phone call.

cards.title: Range Rover Sport L461
cards.meta: 2022–present • D300/D350 (AJ20D6), P400 (AJ20P6), P440e/P510e PHEV, P530 V8 (BMW-derived), SV P635
cards.verdict: The cleanest engine slate a Sport has ever had — no V6 diesel legacy, warranty cover, and values that make any engine bill economically trivial. Early-build electronics niggles, not engines, dominate its enquiries.

engineDatabase.h2: Range Rover Sport Engine Codes — The Complete Database

engines.enquiries: 620 JLR-wide [EM-VERIFIED — recorded as "508PS" in source data; see correction note]
— (aggregated under 306DT in source data — split before citing)

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: 276DT, 306DT, 368DT and 448DT ratings: 94% confidence — four of the highest-volume engines in our dataset [EM-VERIFIED], with the Sport contributing a disproportionate share of their failure enquiries. Petrol V8/V6 ratings: Expert-knowledge tier. L461 powertrains: Provisional — parc too young for volume data. Data note (internal): Same corrections as the Range Rover page, verify at source: (1) the 4.4 SDV8 is 448DT, not "508PS" (508PS is the 5.0 supercharged petrol) — the 620 enquiries logged against "508PS" are shown under 448DT pending verification; (2) AJ20D6 split applied for late-L494/L461 diesels. Addition: the source sheet omitted the L320's petrol V8s, the L494's AJ126 3.0 SC petrol, AJ20P6, and all L461 powertrains — added, marked pattern data/placeholder.

commonProblems.h2: Common Range Rover Sport Engine Problems

problems.description: The Sport's most common catastrophic enquiry [EM-VERIFIED] — and Sports show it earlier in our patterns than gentler-driven Discoverys. Cold-start knock is the warning; £6,500–£9,500 recon the ending [EM-QUOTE]. L320 (2010+) and L494.
Kills the 2.7 outright with little warning — on today's L320 values, it usually kills the car too. 2005–2011.
The 3.6 TDV8's chain and guide wear with snap risk — oil-interval discipline is everything. L320 2007–2010.
Supercharger nose whine and timing chain guide wear on the 5.0 — SVRs live the hardest lives and show it soonest.
Endemic across L320 and L494; compressor burnout presents a fire risk. The most common non-engine enquiry on this model.
Short-run D300/D350s dilute oil through interrupted DPF regens; P400e engines that rarely fire develop their own neglect patterns.

marketIntelligence.h2: Range Rover Sport Market Intelligence — What 711 UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising as the parc exits warranty [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: All three Sport generations rank among our most-enquired models simultaneously [EM-VERIFIED] — no other Land Rover family does that. The Sport generates engine decisions at every price point from £5,000 to £120,000 at once.

L320 enquiries mirror the Freelander pattern — repair-vs-scrap crossroads — while L494 enquiries mirror the Discovery 4: failure-driven, high-stakes, paperwork-dependent. One model page, two completely different reader mindsets. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

Sport-fitted 306DT engines appear in our failure enquiries at lower mileages than the same engine in the Discovery — consistent with harder use, and the reason we weight service history even more heavily on this model. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every Range Rover Sport Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one Range Rover Sport based on this page, make it a late L494 SDV8 or a 2021+ D350 — and treat any sub-£15,000 Sport on a V6 diesel as what it is: an engine gamble with a car attached. Price the gamble, not the car.

replacementCosts.h2: Range Rover Sport Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.figuresNote: 276DT, 306DT, 368DT, 448DT, AJ133, 204PT, AJ20D6 figures [EM-QUOTE]; AJ-V8, AJ126, AJ20P6 and L461 rows [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs Table A].
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: 12–22 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE]. The Sport rule — the tightest maths in the range: A £6,500–£9,500 SDV6 recon fails our 60% scrap-threshold rule on almost every L320 and brushes it on the cheapest early L494s. This is the model where we most often quote three numbers, not one: the recon, the used engine (£3,500–£5,500), and the car's value — because the right answer changes at £12,000, £18,000 and £25,000. Never commit to a Sport engine without all three.

engineEvolution.h2: How the Range Rover Sport Engine Range Evolved — 2005 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: The first Sport was a Discovery 3 in a sharper suit — it inherited the L319's engines and, with them, the oil-pump and chain stories
eras.worthKnowing: 'Sport' was marketing: the L320 weighed over 2.5 tonnes and worked its V6 hard

eras.whyBmwChanged: The L494 shed 400kg+ with the full-size car's aluminium body — the SDV8 arrived and the SVR made it a genuine performance brand
eras.worthKnowing: Same 306DT, lighter car, harder driving: the crank-bearing maths didn't improve as much as the badge suggests

eras.whyBmwChanged: Emissions rules retired the Ford-era V6/V8 diesels — the straight-six Ingeniums closed out the L494 with its cleanest engines
eras.worthKnowing: A 2020–2022 L494 D350 quietly became the pick of the entire generation

eras.whyBmwChanged: Full electrification path — MHEV sixes, big-battery PHEVs, and a BMW-sourced V8 for the flagship
eras.worthKnowing: The first Sport with no high-risk engine anywhere in the range

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a Range Rover Sport?

profiles.verdict: An L494 is a superb family car with genuine pace — the full-size Range Rover's substance at a real discount. Buy the engine, not the wheels.
Short runs are how Sport diesels die — DPF regens interrupted, oil diluted, crank bearings worked cold. Motorway commuters only; town commuters want the P400e or P440e.
3,500kg with the SDV8 or D350 barely noticing — among the best tow cars money can buy.
The single riskiest budget buy on this site. A £9,000 L320 carries engines whose failure costs more than the car — if the fund can't absorb a total loss, it can't absorb this car.
The SVR is a genuine event and the AJ133 is stronger than its whine suggests — budget supercharger and chain-guide attention as a running cost, not a surprise.

faq.h2: Range Rover Sport Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Sport engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Reconditioned units run £3,000–£16,000 supply only depending on generation and engine [EM-QUOTE] — the volume SDV6 sits at £6,500–£9,500, the SDV8 at £8,000–£12,000. Fitting adds £780–£2,100. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which Range Rover Sport engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 4.4 SDV8 (448DT) — the lowest failure-enquiry rate of any big engine in our data. Among current engines, the AJ20D6 D300/D350 is proving similarly strong.

items.question: Which Range Rover Sport engine should I avoid?
items.answer: The 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) without oil-pump history and the 3.0 TDV6/SDV6 (306DT) without documented oil intervals. Both fail catastrophically; on L320 values, both usually total the car.

items.question: Is the Range Rover Sport SDV6 engine reliable?
items.answer: It's the range's defining risk: crankshaft bearing failure at higher mileages, announced by cold-start knock. Sports show it earlier than Discoverys in our patterns — harder lives. Impeccable oil history changes the odds dramatically. Use our diagnostic calculator if yours is knocking.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport engine?
items.answer: Generation decides. L461: always ✅. L494: usually ✅, marginal on the cheapest early cars ⚠️. L320: frequently ❌ for a recon — a used engine or walking away is often the honest answer. Use the Ownership Decision Tool for your car.

items.question: Which Range Rover Sport should I buy?
items.answer: A late L494 SDV8 or 2021+ D350 for the sweet spot; an L461 D350 if buying nearly new; an L320 TDV8 only as an informed enthusiast purchase with a contingency fund.

items.question: How do I find my Range Rover Sport engine code?
items.answer: On the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stampings vary by engine family. Or tell us your variant and year (SDV6, SDV8, D350, P530) and we'll identify it.

items.question: Range Rover Sport vs Range Rover — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Same engines, different lives. The Sport is driven harder and our failure enquiries reflect it — same failure modes, earlier mileages. The full-size car costs more to buy and less per surprise. Buy on engine and history, not badge. Read the full head-to-head →
